verdict: 2026-07-07 11:44:02 PDT — I would satisfy these ACs with exactly this general shape: a small pure `rateTable.js` helper around `normalizeName`, max+1 opaque ids, minimal hydration repair with `{config, changed}`, store actions that call helper upserts, and UI cap affordances derived from the same saved-list contract. The branch mostly follows that shape, uses no new dependency, and does not appear to reinvent an installed library. One persistence-shape issue remains: the dirty-load repair save is layered on top of the generic debounced save effect, so the “save once” invariant is not actually centralized.

findings.title: Dirty hydration repair is saved through two paths
findings.claim: The load effect repairs the server config, calls `setPersisted(fixed)`, then directly calls `saveRates(adapter, fixed)` when `changed`. After the same effect flips `hydratedRef.current = true`, the normal `[persisted, adapter]` persistence effect will see the repaired `persisted` value and schedule the same config for the debounced save path too. That shape violates the Build note’s “one best-effort save” contract for dirty hydration and leaves persistence policy split between two mechanisms.
findings.alternative: Keep the manual dirty-repair save, but record that exact hydrated config as already persisted and have the debounced persistence effect skip once for it; or route dirty hydration through a single persistence helper that both saves and advances the “last saved” marker before `hydratedRef` opens normal user-edit saves.
findings.win: One repaired-load write instead of two, with the no-echo/no-duplicate-save invariant centralized in the store rather than depending on effect timing.
